articles.title: A One-in-a-Million Supernova Appears Five Times — and It Might Settle the Universe's Biggest Debate
articles.summary: Astronomers found a superluminous supernova, SN Winny, that appears as five separate images due to gravitational lensing. By timing the light delays, they can directly measure the Hubble constant, potentially resolving the Hubble tension.

articles.title: The Nuclear Waste Problem No One Wants to Talk About — and Why It's Urgent Now
articles.summary: As nuclear energy gains fresh support from tech giants and governments, the U.S. still lacks a permanent disposal site for its 2,000 annual metric tons of high-level waste. Finland is poised to open the world's first deep geological repository this year.

articles.title: AI Can Boost Critical Thinking — But Only If You Use It at the Right Time
articles.summary: A study presented at the 2026 CHI conference found that participants who used an AI chatbot after partially solving a problem showed better critical thinking than those who used it from the start. Under time pressure, early AI use improved performance but narrowed perspectives.
